Projects Donation ← Back to Schedule

ASI Southwest 2026 Mission Projects

A curated overview of the 2026 project opportunities and ministry initiatives from the official project packet.

FARM STEW

FARM STEW aims to fight extreme poverty and malnutrition in Africa—particularly in Uganda, South Sudan, and Zimbabwe—by training rural families in sustainable agriculture, nutrition, hygiene, and access to clean water. Their holistic "recipe" involves practical, local training to grow food, improve health, and establish income-generating savings groups. Project funding will go toward entering a new area of Renk, South Sudan, with a three-year plan for hands-on farm training with three full-time workers, their training, transport, and supplies to equip families for a more abundant life.

Southwestern Adventist University (SWAU)

Southwestern Adventist University (SWAU) is a private Adventist university in Keene, Texas. Building on a legacy of almost 20 years, SWAU will again be hosting a mission trip for 40 SWAU students to the Navajo Nation. Project funding will go toward routine construction and upkeep projects as well as launching a health seminar. Projects include routine services, five days of outreach, support of Senior Centers, and youth programs.

La Vida Mission

La Vida Mission serves the Navajo Nation for the past 60 years. Its primary mission is to provide education through a K-8 boarding school and share the gospel of Jesus, focusing on community outreach, offering free education, a free water supply to local families, and a local church. Project funding will go toward purchasing a multi-use tractor for agricultural, road, and sewer work, as well as for pulling vehicles that get stuck.

Build and Restore International

Build and Restore International mobilizes volunteers from around the globe to build and restore churches, schools, and other facilities. Through these projects, they seek to enhance the physical and spiritual well-being of communities, fostering hope and transforming lives. Project funds will go toward renovation of a Houston, Texas area project to create a learning center to include a lifestyle center, trade academy, and farm to provide education, trades, oxygen therapy, massage, and health coaching to those in need.

Narrow Way Ministries

Narrow Way Ministries is a full-spectrum faith ministry of message, mission, and music, focused on delivering individuals from the LGBT+ lifestyle. Project funds will support a stand-alone server to host a repository of testimonies from the recent full-feature documentary on religious liberty, freedom of speech, and conversion therapy.

Creation Illustrated

Creation Illustrated ministry is dedicated to the eternal impact of sharing Biblical truth and character-building lessons with the purpose of sharing the wonders of God’s creation. By revealing fresh insights of His infinite wisdom, gentle touch, undeniable justice, redeeming love, and flawless design, pure truth shall bring renewed peace. Project funding will go toward distributing Creation Illustrated magazines (and back issues) to prison inmates with the help of Chaplains and Ministry relationships.

Profeteia Ministries

Profeteia Ministries is dedicated to proclaiming the gospel and biblical prophecy through digital platforms in Spanish. The ministry was founded in Argentina and has developed a significant audience of 120k subscribers and some videos with over 1MM views, including a growing presence in the United States. Project funding will go toward creating a free digital Bible Study platform (on twelve foundational Adventist teachings) that guides individuals from Bible studies to connection with a local SDA church.

Ouachita Hills Academy (OHA)

Ouachita Hills Academy (OHA) is a Seventh-day Adventist supporting ministry school located in southwest Arkansas. The lovely, natural setting of the campus and the peaceful surroundings provide a welcome retreat for students and staff alike. The mission of OHA is to nurture its students in a personal relationship with Jesus, to educate them for a life of service, and to train them to take the gospel to the world. Project funding will go toward purchasing multiple cameras as part of a security system to deter crime, provide protection, and provide accountability.

Ouachita Hills College (OHC)

Ouachita Hills College (OHC) is a self-supporting Seventh-day Adventist educational organization. OHC exists to provide a Christ-centered, character-oriented education that equips students to be successful in their chosen profession, to be active soul winners, and to demonstrate the power of implementing God’s counsel for the Seventh-day Adventist church. Project funding will go toward cafeteria upgrades, including new fans, a P.A. system, and wall sound tiles to support other meetings and uses of the cafeteria.

Child Impact International

Child Impact International is committed to protecting vulnerable children through education, prevention, and long-term care initiatives that address the root causes of exploitation. Guided by our mission to empower vulnerable children through education and fight to eradicate exploitation, our programs focus on equipping children, families, and communities with the knowledge and tools necessary to prevent abuse before it occurs. Project funds will go toward a new ministry for providing workshops, along with materials and teaching resources, to educate vulnerable children in public schools and communities about child protection. These workshops provide age-appropriate instruction that helps children understand safe and unsafe touch, recognize inappropriate behavior, establish personal boundaries, and identify trusted adults and reporting pathways. Through our Sexual Abuse Prevention Outreach, we are expanding educational workshops to reach all 106 public schools in the municipality of Barreirinha, Brazil over an 18-month period.
